One of the surprising findings in coding agents has been the relative *ineffectiveness* of multi-agent systems for large tasks, e.g. https://t.co/oqmfj5SdKG

The below paper was our attempt to take a look at the problem, and I think we have some interesting results!

Big lesson from high reliability organizations that AI agent builders need to learn is reliability is the property of systems. Current agentic tools are weaker than the agents: they are bad at agent-agent handoffs, escalation, when to call in humans. All keys to high reliability.

Really excited for the things @rosstaylor90 has been building to go out into the world. He's been one of the people I can always rely on to have non-cope takes on what we need to do to make the open ecosystem great.

Software horror: litellm PyPI supply chain attack. 

Simple `pip install litellm` was enough to exfiltrate SSH keys, AWS/GCP/Azure creds, Kubernetes configs, git credentials, env vars (all your API keys), shell history, crypto wallets, SSL private keys, CI/CD secrets, database

OpenAI finished training its latest model, codenamed Spud. It’s going to be big  & has renamed its product org “AGI Deployment.” Meanwhile, people still debate that AI has not reached AGI level or can’t do this or that. The era of human foolishness and coping is about to end. 🤖

All the AI labs are exploring the opportunity space for LLMs. Anthropic has always been very focused and OpenAI more prone to testing, and abandoning, concepts (GPT Store, Sora, etc.). Google sort of does everything at once. It is unclear which strategy will be best in the end.
